entities.name: COMMERCIALIZATION
entities.description: The process of managing or running something principally for financial gain, specifically regarding agricultural crops

entities.name: WOMEN
entities.description: Human females, specifically wives in married households in the study

entities.name: MEN
entities.description: Human males, specifically husbands in married households in the study

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop studied for its commercialization and impact on gender dynamics

entities.name: PHALOMBE DISTRICT
entities.description: A district in Southern Malawi where the study was conducted

entities.name: SOUTHERN MALAWI
entities.description: The region in Malawi where the study took place

entities.name: EXPERIMENTAL GAME
entities.description: A tool used to identify preferences for the intra-household distribution of income

entities.name: INTRA-HOUSEHOLD DISTRIBUTION OF INCOME
entities.description: The way income generated from a crop is shared between members of a household

entities.name: MATRILINEAL SYSTEM OF INHERITANCE
entities.description: A system where inheritance is passed down through the female line

entities.name: MATRILOCAL MARRIAGE
entities.description: A marriage custom where the couple resides with or near the wife's family

entities.name: BARGAINING POWER
entities.description: The relative ability of women to influence decision-making within the household

relationships.description: Commercialization is applied to the production of pigeonpea
The effect of commercialization on the empowerment of women was tested
Pigeonpea is the crop studied in Phalombe district
Phalombe district is located in Southern Malawi
Experimental game was used to identify preferences for income distribution
Matrilineal inheritance strengthens the bargaining power of women
Matrilocal marriage strengthens the bargaining power of women
Bargaining power is an attribute of women in this social context
Bargaining power and social context determine the outcome of commercialization
